reviews.author: Barsbold Baatarsuren
reviews.text: Da Lat Railway Station (Ga Đà Lạt) is such a nice and charming place to visit. The architecture is beautiful, and the atmosphere feels calm and nostalgic. What made the visit special for our family was the story behind the station. Listening to the history and sharing it with our kids made them really happy and curious. It turned the visit into a fun learning experience, not just sightseeing. A great stop for families, history lovers, and anyone who enjoys places with character and meaning. Highly recommended when visiting Da Lat. 🚂✨

reviews.author: Wong TF
reviews.text: 🚂🚂🚂 A must itinerary for 1st timer to Dalat. Quaint 30 minutes train trip (one way) where you will be entertained by a saxophonist along the way (see last video - with sound) from Dalat to Trai Mat & return. Reminder for those 1st timer: 1. If you are likely to be annoyed by the slightly loud & almost continuous melodious music from the saxophone, bring along a pair of ear plugs; 2. If you are buying returned ticket (better buy as it will be cheaper to buy to & fro tickets, VND 124K as of Dec., 2025, buying from certain apps might be cheaper), buy the return ticket with time of about 2 hours after your arrival time at Trai Mat (see the time table in the attached photos). This will give you ample time to explore Linh Phuoc Pagoda & Trai Mat Town (unless you are not interested in these); 3. While in the moving train, go outside towards at the both ends of the train doors to have different perspective of surrouding view compared with view from inside the cabin (be careful not to drop your mobile phone); 4. There are free warm artichoke tea inside the train.

reviews.author: Joe Nguyen
reviews.text: First time to visit the old railway station in Dalat was great, even though I have been to Dalat many times. Well maintain facilities, great old train tour, lovely free artichoke tea, clean toilet and helpful staffs. They might need to improve more for landscaping all around which still have a lot of potential, perhaps some playground, picnic areas, garden et cetera

reviews.author: Sarah
reviews.text: Interesting little visit during the day, easy walk from Da lat centre. Documentary room was very interesting ( although subtitles are a little random). All the carriage doors were locked. Couldn't work out if any trains were running, the timetable suggested they are but we didn't see anything at the set times. No staff seen after the ticket office in car park.

reviews.author: Malaysian Malaysia
reviews.text: The overall experience is ok but the ticket seller is not in advising tourist, we bought tickets for 2pm train and he advised us to return by 3.10pm but the journey took 30 mins and we dun have time to spend at linh phouc temple, we need to rush to catch the train and unable to finish touring the temple. He shall advise us to take the later return train. Overall good scenic and we took some photos at the station.

reviews.author: S L Chong
reviews.text: We dropped by this historic train station. There is a running train for tourists although we didn't ride on it but saw the train and carriages. There are some cafes in static wagons. We took photos and got some souvenirs here.

reviews.author: Remko
reviews.text: Old trainstation with long history. Has some old trains. Can buy souvenirs, coffee and snacks. Not very special

reviews.author: Alfons Grabher
reviews.text: An interesting place to see how first advancements into a modern age were made, and how they were forsaken and wasted. The tourist train ride takes you through the densely build sheds and intense farming constructs, which are littered with waste. There’s loud honking and very loud musical instruments being played ineptly throughout the train ride, bring NC earplugs if you’re sensitive. A bit overpriced.

reviews.author: Hiếu Huỳnh
reviews.text: Da Lat Train Station remains as charming as ever, preserving the beauty of the past. I booked a night round-trip train ride, and the scenery along the way was truly captivating. Adding to the atmosphere, there was live violin music performed in the carriage, which made the journey even more memorable. Overall, it was a delightful and worthwhile experience!

reviews.author: Peter Cook
reviews.text: Interesting place to visit when in Da Lat. Worth spending half a day here to take a return trip up the line.

reviews.author: David McLeod
reviews.text: Pleasant railway station and bit of history left over from the French. The rail journey itself is slow meandering in the best way, while there is live music on board to set the atmosphere.

reviews.author: Wuezellyn Juviole
reviews.text: I arrived very early and ended up being the first customer of the day, no crowds, no lines, just peaceful surroundings. The weather was sunny, and the clear sky made the station look even more picturesque. While there wasn’t much to explore, the visit still felt exciting thanks to the charm of this vintage railway station. That said, it’s the kind of place I’d probably visit just once, mainly for the photo opportunity.

reviews.author: Bridget
reviews.text: Beautiful old station with charming French architecture and vintage trains. Great for photos and a quick stroll. There's a small entrance fee, but it's worth it for the unique vibe and history.

reviews.author: David Ribovski
reviews.text: I thought it was kind of sad. Nice trains but the place is very run down, neglected. We only spent 15 min there. Some good photo ops.

reviews.author: Patrick Seo
reviews.text: It's a train station that has historical significance in Vietnam, but it doesn't approach us in a special sense. It's just a place to sit in a shade chair for a while and avoid the heat. A charming Vietnamese housewife modeled me beautifully, but I can't find an Instagram account, so I just share it here. If it's you, please DM me.

reviews.author: Suad Alsanad
reviews.text: The room is good, but the reception is small and the location of this place in a Narrow road

reviews.author: Yann Ling Tai
reviews.text: Entrance ticket VND 50,000 per person. Very Nostalgia place. The side cafe coffee and avocado is very nice.

reviews.author: Tran Thuan
reviews.text: It’s actually a very nice place to take pictures and enjoy a little of old Dalat vibe with nostalgia corner and classic street music performance. I don’t recommend every one to take the train to Trai Mat, the whole trip was dull and all you see along the way is house after houses, not a single scenic view.

reviews.author: YongTae kim
reviews.text: Price is 10 time higher than 2024. Now VND 50,000 pax. It is not valuable. Just old style train station. Dont understand why increases 50k from 5k? I don't recommend to visit. Just waste time and money even though it is small.

reviews.author: Maik M
reviews.text: 5k for parking, decent place with light for some pictures. It takes probably about 15' and you will see everything. Souvenirs available with a lovely cafe within the campus. Place tends to get busy with tourists so better visit during weekends.

reviews.author: Jim Reaugh
reviews.text: This place is like stepping into a time machine, and I'm not just saying that because of the vintage steam locomotive rides—although, let's be honest, that's a big draw. Built back in the '30s during the French colonial period, this station isn't just any old stop; it's a piece of architectural history. The design screams Art Deco with a twist, featuring three high triangular peaks that nod to the Lang Biang Mountains. It's like the building itself is trying to tell you, "Hey, we've got style and history!" Even though the days of regular train services are long gone, the station still offers a charming short ride to Trai Mat. It's perfect for those who want to relive the golden age of train travel, or at least snap a few Instagram-worthy photos. Plus, the journey offers stunning views of the countryside that you just can't get anywhere else. It's a delightful blend of culture, history & a dash of old-world charm.

reviews.author: Nick Pouchkareff
reviews.text: An interesting part of the French colonial past. Worth a visit. A films plays the story of the railway. Part of the history of Vietnam and certainly railways around the world.

reviews.author: duchess anastacio
reviews.text: Da Lat Railway station entrance fee is like the price of going to toilet, 5K VND only for one person. Super cheap but you'll have instagrammable pics inside. You'll see how the old trains looks like and you can take a picture at the railway. Also, souvenir made of wood are available in the area. Famous souvenir is the honk of the train. Mostly picturesque. CR here is not recommendable since a bit public and no maintenance. You could take your lunch at the café and restay available. We went here thru grab app and very accessible.

reviews.author: Sana Cho
reviews.text: It was a pretty affordable attraction and it’s fun to take some pics with the train. There’s a couple of small cafes in the train that are reasonably priced. Such a cute bright building!

reviews.author: Intan Setyari
reviews.text: its just nice and worth to gisit when you are in dalat, but nothing much you can do. heard that you can took the train but it was too late when we going there, still the place was cute and nice ;)

reviews.author: Lukáš Volf
reviews.text: The old station building does not serve its original purpose, as you will learn further from any guidebook. what you won't know, however, is its current purpose. After paying the entrance fee, which was 10,000 VND, you have the opportunity to admire the colonial architecture. From the outside, the building is quite well preserved. in the main hall today you will only find a market with souvenirs. The accessible platforms have individual compartments parked where many visitors take photos. The interior of some coupes is interesting. If you walk about 150 meters along the platform, you will come across a modern cafe on the left, which is set in a community garden. 30 minutes is plenty for a visit.

reviews.author: Louis Tye
reviews.text: To be frank, the authority raised the entrance ticket from 5k vnd to 50k vnd which i find is rediculous. (10 times increment is too much to do so) , this round saw less crowd and the whole compound feel deserted. The Choco cafe had face serious loss of customers due to 50k entry fees. I can see that this place is meaningless for me to revisit anymore.

reviews.author: Hữu Lâm
reviews.text: The train station in Da Lat is a charming and historic site that offers a unique travel experience. The station's French colonial architecture and classic steam trains transport you back in time to a bygone era. The station is well-maintained and the staff are friendly and helpful, providing information and assistance to travelers. The train ride itself is a scenic and picturesque journey through the lush forests and rolling hills of the Central Highlands, with stunning views of the countryside. The ticket prices are reasonable, making it an affordable way to explore the area. However, it's important to note that train schedules can be limited, and it's recommended to book tickets in advance to avoid disappointment. Overall, the train station in Da Lat is a must-visit for those seeking a unique and unforgettable travel experience in Vietnam!!!!

reviews.author: QUACKSERS duck
reviews.text: It's ok I guess, pretty small and your able to explore everything pretty fast but the cafe there is pretty good and you can buy roasted coffee beans and chocolate which tastes really good

reviews.author: Charles Cheung
reviews.text: Good thing to do in Dalat, can see the historical of the railway station and the trains. But the place is quite small, the entry ticket is not expensive but not reasonable.
